steps.title: Identify what the task requires
steps.content: The student wants to “present historical analysis to an audience unfamiliar with the concept.” This signals a need to explain or define the concept itself, not compare approaches or show an application.

steps.title: Apply this to the choices
steps.content: Choice C directly defines historical analysis as “an approach to art criticism” that “considers the historical context in which a work was created,” giving an unfamiliar audience the concept's meaning and method. Choices A and B focus on texture analysis or comparison instead of explaining historical analysis, while choice D shows only an example without teaching the concept. That matches choice C — C is correct.
